concepts.name: Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A tree data structure where each node has at most two children, referred to as the left child and the right child.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit of a tree, containing data and references to its children.

concepts.name: Root Node
concepts.short_definition: The topmost node in a binary tree, from which all other nodes descend.

concepts.name: Child Node
concepts.short_definition: A node directly connected to another node (the parent) and below it in the tree hierarchy.

concepts.name: Parent Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that has one or more child nodes directly below it in the tree hierarchy.

concepts.name: Leaf Node
concepts.short_definition: A node with no children.

concepts.name: Internal Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that has at least one child.

concepts.name: Left Child
concepts.short_definition: The child node located to the left of its parent.

concepts.name: Right Child
concepts.short_definition: The child node located to the right of its parent.

concepts.name: Height
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges on the longest path from the root to a leaf node.

concepts.name: Depth of Node
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges from the root to that node.

concepts.name: Level
concepts.short_definition: The distance of a node from the root. The root is at level 0.

concepts.name: Full Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree in which every node has either 0 or 2 children.

concepts.name: Complete Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree in which all levels are completely filled except possibly the last level, which is filled from left to right.

concepts.name: Perfect Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree in which all internal nodes have two children and all leaves are at the same level.

concepts.name: Balanced Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree where the height difference between the left and right subtrees of any node is at most 1.

concepts.name: Skewed Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree where each node has only one child, resembling a linked list.

concepts.name: Binary Search Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree where for each node, all values in the left subtree are less than the node's value, and all values in the right subtree are greater.

concepts.name: Tree Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each node in a tree exactly once.

concepts.name: In-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Traverses the left subtree, then the root, then the right subtree.

concepts.name: Pre-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Traverses the root, then the left subtree, then the right subtree.

concepts.name: Post-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Traverses the left subtree, then the right subtree, then the root.

concepts.name: Level-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Traverses the tree level by level, from left to right.

concepts.name: Insertion
concepts.short_definition: The process of adding a new node to a binary tree.

concepts.name: Deletion
concepts.short_definition: The process of removing a node from a binary tree.

concepts.name: Search
concepts.short_definition: The process of locating a specific node within a binary tree.

concepts.name: Tree Representation
concepts.short_definition: Methods for storing a binary tree in memory, such as using arrays or linked structures.

concepts.name: Array Representation
concepts.short_definition: Representing a binary tree using an array, often for complete binary trees.

concepts.name: Linked Representation
concepts.short_definition: Representing a binary tree using nodes with pointers to their children.

concepts.name: Tree Size
concepts.short_definition: The total number of nodes in a binary tree.

concepts.name: Empty Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree with no nodes.

concepts.name: Subtree
concepts.short_definition: A tree formed by a node and all of its descendants.

concepts.name: Ancestor
concepts.short_definition: A node that is on the path from the root to another node.

concepts.name: Descendant
concepts.short_definition: A node that is in the subtree of another node.

concepts.name: Sibling
concepts.short_definition: Nodes that share the same parent.

concepts.name: Threaded Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree where null pointers are replaced with pointers to inorder successor or predecessor nodes.

concepts.name: Expression Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree used to represent mathematical expressions.

concepts.name: Huffman Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree used for lossless data compression.

concepts.name: Heap
concepts.short_definition: A specialized tree-based data structure that satisfies the heap property (min-heap or max-heap).
